2606.12332.title: Measuring Semantic Progress in Multi-turn Dialogue via Information Gain
2606.12332.paper_summary: Evaluating multi-turn dialogue is challenging because quality emerges across turns rather than within individual responses. We focus on a key dimension of information-seeking dialogue: semantic progress, defined as the accumulation of new, question-relevant, and non-redundant information over the course of a conversation. We formalize semantic progress as question-conditioned uncertainty reduction and introduce an information-theoretic metric that approximates it in embedding space. Our main estimator uses a tractable Gaussian formulation with closed-form updates, while a complementary maximum-entropy argument shows why log-determinant structure arises more broadly when only second-order embedding information is retained. This formulation yields desirable theoretical properties, including monotonicity, additive decomposition of total information gain across turns, and diminishing returns for redundant evidence. Unlike LLM-as-a-judge approaches, our metric requires no autoregressive inference at evaluation time and is fully reproducible for a fixed embedding model. Experiments on MT-Bench, Chatbot Arena, and UltraFeedback show that the proposed metric achieves competitive agreement with human judgments despite targeting only semantic progress, with improved alignment on MT-Bench and UltraFeedback compared to several LLM-based judges. Notably, the method remains effective with lightweight embedding models under CPU-only execution, indicating that semantic progress can be captured without reliance on large model capacity.
2606.12332.paper_summary_zh: 評估多輪對話是具有挑戰性的，因為質量是在多輪之間出現，而不是在單個回應中。我們專注於信息尋求對話的一個關鍵維度：語義進展，定義為在對話過程中累積新的、與問題相關的且不冗餘的信息。我們將語義進展形式化為基於問題的不確定性減少，並引入一種信息論度量，該度量在嵌入空間中近似它。我們的主要估計器使用可處理的高斯形式，並具有封閉形式的更新，而一個補充的最大熵論證顯示為什麼當僅保留二階嵌入信息時，對數行列式結構會更廣泛地出現。這一形式化產生了理想的理論特性，包括單調性、跨輪次的總信息增益的可加分解，以及對冗餘證據的遞減回報。與LLM作為評判者的方法不同，我們的度量在評估時不需要自回歸推斷，並且對於固定的嵌入模型是完全可重現的。在MT-Bench、Chatbot Arena和UltraFeedback上的實驗顯示，所提出的度量在僅針對語義進展的情況下，與人類判斷達成了競爭性的一致性，並且在MT-Bench和UltraFeedback上與幾個基於LLM的評判者相比，對齊得到了改善。值得注意的是，該方法在僅使用CPU執行的輕量級嵌入模型下仍然有效，這表明語義進展可以在不依賴大型模型容量的情況下被捕捉。
2606.12332.author: Paul He et.al.
2606.12332.authors: Paul He, Shiva Kasiviswanathan, Dominik Janzing
